blocks.md: So far every array held plain numbers. APL also lets an item *be* another array. Write vectors side by side in parentheses to nest them.
`≡` (depth) reports how deeply nested an array is. A plain number is depth 0, a flat vector depth 1, a vector of vectors depth 2.
To reach inside, `↑` (first) returns the first item, and dyadic `n⊃` (pick) selects the n-th item. `¨` (each) maps a function over every item.

blocks.prompt: Report the depth of the nested vector (1 2)(3 4 5)(6). Expect 2.
blocks.hint: a vector whose items are vectors has depth 2

blocks.prompt: Take the first item of (10 20 30)(40 50), giving 10 20 30.
Pick the second item of (10 20 30)(40 50), giving 40 50.
Count the total number of elements across the items of (1 2 3 4)(5 6)(7 8 9) by summing the length of each. Expect 9.
blocks.hint: ≢¨ gives a length per item; +/ adds them
